methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Calogena is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Frédéric Mariotte that has raised €100M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 peers across nuclear energy: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The peer-median approach is not applicable in this instance. The nuclear energy sector, while containing 8 same-sector private comparables, does not yield a sufficiently dense peer set when filtered by stage and valuation tier. Same-stage and adjacent-stage peers number only 2 combined, falling short of the 3-point minimum required for meaningful median analysis. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable methodological error given nuclear energy's distinct capital intensity, regulatory profile, and technology risk relative to broader frontier tech. No triangulated valuation range can therefore be supported by the available data.

The primary constraint is data sparsity within Calogena's narrow segment: early-stage nuclear technology companies remain limited in number, and public comparable trading multiples do not reliably anchor early-stage private valuations in this sector. Forward analysis would benefit from either additional Series A or late-seed nuclear peers with disclosed valuations, or a shift to alternative methods such as DCF or cost-of-capital approaches if management projections become available. At present, the analysis cannot responsibly produce a valuation range.
